title: Amii alimente la dynamique de l’IA au Canada alors que Upper Bound ouvre ses portes avec une croissance de 53 % et 11 000 participants internationaux
author: Not Specified
description: Amii's Upper Bound conference, Canada's fastest-growing AI event, opened its fifth annual edition in Edmonton with 11,000 attendees from 22 countries and sold out a month in advance. The event achieved 53% growth year-over-year and featured participation from federal and provincial leaders alongside major tech companies, while showcasing AI workforce training initiatives supported by Google and the Canadian government.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Google is highlighted as a major industry leader presenting at the conference and supporting Amii's AI Workforce Readiness initiative with $5 million in funding, demonstrating commitment to AI talent development in Canada.

Google is highlighted as a major industry leader presenting at the conference and supporting Amii's AI Workforce Readiness initiative with $5 million in funding, demonstrating commitment to AI talent development in Canada.
Meta is listed among the major tech giants presenting and intervening at the conference, showing engagement with Canada's AI community and research initiatives.
Electronic Arts is mentioned as a major industry presenter at Upper Bound, showing involvement in the broader AI ecosystem and innovation landscape.

title: Amii 助推加拿大人工智能发展势头，Upper Bound 大会盛大开幕，规模增长 53%，吸引 11,000 名全球参会者
author: Globe Newswire
description: The Alberta Machine Intelligence Institute (Amii) launched its fifth annual Upper Bound AI conference in Edmonton on May 19, 2026, attracting 11,000 researchers, business leaders, and policymakers from 22 countries—a 53% year-over-year increase. The conference features government and industry leaders, including executives from Google, Anthropic, Meta, Sony AI, Electronic Arts, and Mozilla. Amii is expanding Canada's AI talent pipeline through initiatives like the Google.org-backed AI Workforce Readiness program ($5M CAD) and the government-funded AI Pathways program ($10.4M CAD).

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Google is highlighted as a major industry partner supporting Amii's AI Workforce Readiness program with $5M CAD investment, demonstrating commitment to AI talent development and positioning as a key player in Canada's AI ecosystem.

Google is highlighted as a major industry partner supporting Amii's AI Workforce Readiness program with $5M CAD investment, demonstrating commitment to AI talent development and positioning as a key player in Canada's AI ecosystem.
Meta is recognized as a leading industry participant providing insights at the conference, demonstrating engagement with the broader AI research and development community.
Electronic Arts is highlighted as a participating industry leader, indicating engagement with AI innovation and the broader AI ecosystem development.

title: Amii beflügelt Kanadas KI-Dynamik: Upper Bound eröffnet mit 53 % Wachstum und 11.000 Teilnehmern aus aller Welt
author: Not Specified
description: Amii's fifth annual Upper Bound AI conference opened with 11,000 attendees from 22 countries, marking a 53% increase from the previous year. The event sold out a month in advance and featured participation from Canadian government ministers and major tech companies including Google, Anthropic, Meta, Sony AI, Electronic Arts, and Mozilla. The conference showcases Canada's growing position as a global AI innovation hub and includes training initiatives supported by Google.org and the Canadian government.

title: Amii Fuels Canada’s AI Momentum as Upper Bound Opens with 53% Growth and 11,000 Global Attendees
author: Amii (Alberta Machine Intelligence Institute)
description: Amii's Upper Bound AI conference held its fifth annual event in Edmonton on May 19-22, 2026, attracting 11,000 attendees from 22 countries with a 53% year-over-year growth. The event featured government and industry leaders, including Canadian federal and provincial ministers, and representatives from major tech companies. The conference highlighted Canada's AI talent pipeline initiatives, including a $5M Google-supported AI Workforce Readiness program and a $10.4M Government of Canada AI Pathways initiative for energy workers.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Google is highlighted as a major supporter of Amii's AI Workforce Readiness initiative with $5M funding, demonstrating commitment to AI talent development and positioning the company as a key player in Canada's AI ecosystem.

Google is highlighted as a major supporter of Amii's AI Workforce Readiness initiative with $5M funding, demonstrating commitment to AI talent development and positioning the company as a key player in Canada's AI ecosystem.
Meta is listed among global industry leaders providing representation and insights at Upper Bound, signaling active engagement in Canada's AI innovation ecosystem.
Electronic Arts is featured among global industry leaders at the conference, showing engagement with the broader AI innovation and business adoption landscape.
